text:
Once upon a time, there was a curious cat who lived in an old oak tree. Its name was Felix. Felix loved to explore the forest. Every day, Felix would leap down into the undergrowth. There he found butterflies, mushrooms, and all sorts of tiny critters. One afternoon, Felix met a porcupine.

The porcupine was named Pop. Pop had vibrant quills of every color. Felix admired their beauty. Pop listened intently while Felix talked about his adventures. They soon became good friends.

One day, the forest shook. A great storm had come. Lightning struck near Felix's tree, throwing branches across the ground. Rain poured loudly. Pop quivered nervously.

Suddenly, Felix thought of an ingenious idea. He waved his paws and started collecting sticks. Soon, he had a little bridge.

With careful hops on his makeshift bridge, Felix carried Pop safely across the muddy floodwaters. They danced across the puddle together without a single quill prick
